stem: In a triangle, two sides have lengths \( [[a]] \) and \( [[b]] \), and the angle between them measures \( [[deg]]^\circ \). Find the length of the third side.
solution: By the Law of Cosines, \( c^2 = [[a]]^2 + [[b]]^2 - 2\cdot[[a]]\cdot[[b]]\cos([[deg]]^\circ) \). Taking the positive root gives \( c = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This just adds the two given sides.
This takes the difference of the two sides.
This uses \( \sqrt{a^2+b^2} \), as if the included angle were \( 90^\circ \).
This uses \( +ab \) inside the root (a wrong cross-term).
This uses \( -ab \) inside the root (a wrong cross-term).
This adds the cross term \( 2ab\cos C \) instead of subtracting it.
This is off by one from the correct length.

stem: A triangle has sides of length \( [[a]] \), \( [[b]] \), and \( [[c]] \). Find the measure (in radians) of the angle opposite the side of length \( [[c]] \).
solution: By the Law of Cosines, \( c^2 = a^2+b^2-2ab\cos C \), so \( \cos C = \dfrac{a^2+b^2-c^2}{2ab} \) and \( C = \arccos\dfrac{([[a]])^2+([[b]])^2-([[c]])^2}{2\cdot[[a]]\cdot[[b]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This finds the angle opposite side \( b \) instead of side \( c \).
This finds the angle opposite side \( a \) instead of side \( c \).
This adds \( c^2 \) instead of subtracting it in the numerator.
This uses the supplementary angle instead of the correct one.

stem: Two hikers start at the same point. One walks \( [[a]] \) miles due along one trail, and the other walks \( [[b]] \) miles along a second trail that diverges from the first at an angle of \( [[deg]]^\circ \). How far apart are the hikers?
solution: The hikers' paths and the segment between them form a triangle with two known sides and the included angle. By the Law of Cosines, the distance is \( d=\sqrt{[[a]]^2+[[b]]^2-2\cdot[[a]]\cdot[[b]]\cdot\cos([[deg]]^\circ)} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This just adds the two distances walked.
This takes the difference of the two distances.
This uses \( \sqrt{a^2+b^2} \), as if the trails diverged at \( 90^\circ \).
This uses \( +ab \) inside the root (a wrong cross-term).
This uses \( -ab \) inside the root (a wrong cross-term).
This adds the cross term \( 2ab\cos C \) instead of subtracting it.
